tx.en: Feeling nervous is normal. It means your brain is ready.

story.en: Your body sends butterflies in your stomach. That is energy, not danger.

tx.en: Slow inhale... slow exhale. Let your body settle

inf.en: This lowers heart rate and helps your brain focus clearly

q.en: What is the best way to handle nervous feelings?

tx.en: Notice your body without judging it

inf.en: Helps the brain stop alarm reactions
Builds emotional control before performance

tx.en: Fear is just a signal, not a command

story.en: Fear appears before important moments. It does not mean something is wrong.

tx.en: Breathe slowly and keep your shoulders relaxed

inf.en: Reduces muscle tension and improves mental clarity

q.en: What should you do when fear appears?

tx.en: Let fear be there, but don’t follow it

inf.en: Teaches separation between emotion and action
Builds calm nervous system response

tx.en: Focus on one question at a time

story.en: You don’t need to know everything at once. You only need the next answer.

inf.en: Activates focus and reduces mental overload

q.en: What is the best strategy in an exam?

op: Think of all questions at once
Do one question at a time

tx.en: Stay with only this question in front of you

inf.en: Improves attention and reduces anxiety loops
Helps maintain attention stability

tx.en: MISSION 39: WHEN A QUESTION IS HARD

story.en: Some questions feel difficult at first, but your knowledge is already inside you.

tx.en: Slow breath to restart your thinking

inf.en: Helps reset cognitive stress and improves recall

q.en: What should you do if a question is hard?

tx.en: Trust your memory and move step by step

inf.en: Builds problem decomposition skills
Improves confidence in reasoning

tx.en: MISSION 40: WHEN YOU FEEL ALONE IN EXAMS
You are stronger than the moment

story.en: Even if you feel pressure, you still have your knowledge and preparation.

tx.en: Slow breathing to bring comfort back

inf.en: Activates calming systems and reduces stress perception

op: I will try step by step

inf.en: Builds emotional resilience
Reinforces self-support system

story.en: Success is not about perfection. It is about showing what you know.

tx.en: Breathe and relax your face muscles

inf.en: Reduces tension and improves cognitive performance

q.en: What matters most in an exam?

inf.en: Builds healthy achievement mindset
Supports steady performance under stress

tx.en: No matter what happened, you continue growing

story.en: One exam does not define you. Learning is a long journey.

tx.en: Slow recovery breathing to relax your body

inf.en: Helps release stress hormones after performance

q.en: What is the best reaction after finishing?

inf.en: Restores emotional balance and reduces cognitive fatigue
Completes stress cycle reset
